source_jobs.title: Teaching Assistant - English
source_jobs.description:
MyEdSpace is an education technology platform that connects the best teachers on the planet with the students who need them the most - wherever they are, whatever their means.

Backed by $15m in Series A funding from some of the leading investors in the space, we're ready to scale - and we want you to help us build and scale MyEdSpace as part of a collaborative and ambitious team.

We're on a mission to make a world-class education accessible to all.

We believe that every child deserves access to quality education, regardless of geography or socioeconomic background, to achieve their dreams and build a brighter future.

If you’re motivated by solving meaningful problems and creating real impact through your work on a societal level and make a real impact on the world - we’d love to hear from you!

We're hiring a full time Teaching Assistant for our UK based English teaching team!




ABOUT THE ROLE

 - Available to both current university students and those on a gap year.

 - Role involves collaborating closely with teachers to assist with course development, including creation of homework questions, converting lesson slides to workbooks and storing video solutions

 - Engages in the development of educational content and tools.

 - This is a full time hybrid role based in London, with 2-3 days a week in the office.





RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Transforming lessons into workbooks for student use.

 - Generating homework questions to complement our English teachers' lessons.

 - Participating in the live chat of livestreamed English lessons to provide subject-related responses to student questions as well as adherence to MyEdSpace codes of conduct.

 - Archiving teachers' video solutions post-filming.

 - Updating the online platform for easy student access before lessons.





WHO WE'RE SEEKING

 - Grade A*/A at A-Level in a humanities subject and a grade 9/8 at GCSE in English Literature and English Language.

 - Ability to work collaboratively with educators and adapt to curriculum needs.

 - Excellent organisational and communication skills.

 - Committed, flexible, and eager to engage in educational content development.

 - Ability to work 5 days a week with a flexible working pattern that involves evening work on some days to support lesson moderation.



✨ Our values

PUT INTEGRITY FIRST Honesty matters. Tell the truth, and be straight-up. Be transparent and do the right thing. This builds respect and reliability for our students, families and team members.

KNOW YOUR COORDINATES Understand where you are and where you want to get to. Know your strengths and acknowledge your gaps. Think from first principles. Question things, and never pretend to know what you don't.

RAISE THE BAR Own it and get it done. Do better. Iterate quickly and seek feedback. Deliver real value. Be excellent and lift others up. Share learnings and help your teammates improve.

LOVE TO BE WRONG Push boundaries. Be outside your comfort zone. Naturally you'll get some things wrong and that's okay. Embrace feedback. Learn from it and get closer to excellence.

WIN TOGETHER To achieve our mission, we have to work together. We all have a role to play, so help each other get there. We’re stronger as a team so inspire, support and respect each other.



🙋 Why you’ll love working here

We’re a team on a mission to transform education for the better. Joining MES means you’ll be part of something ambitious, fast-moving, and full of purpose. Here’s what you can expect:

 - The chance to make a real impact: your work directly shapes the future of education.

 - A fast-paced and high-growth environment where ideas move quickly and careers accelerate.

 - A collaborative, supportive culture: we’re head quartered in the UK, but we’re a global team with colleagues in 15+ countries, bringing a rich mix of perspectives and energy.



We are committed to building a diverse team and strongly encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds. We believe diverse p

source_jobs.title: Live Lesson Moderator - Physics
source_jobs.description:
MyEdSpace is an education technology platform that connects the best teachers on the planet with the students who need them the most - wherever they are, whatever their means.

Backed by $15m in Series A funding from some of the leading investors in the space, we're ready to scale - and we want you to help us build and scale MyEdSpace as part of a collaborative and ambitious team.

We're on a mission to make a world-class education accessible to all.

We believe that every child deserves access to quality education, regardless of geography or socioeconomic background, to achieve their dreams and build a brighter future.

If you’re motivated by solving meaningful problems and creating real impact through your work on a societal level and make a real impact on the world - we’d love to hear from you!

We're hiring a Live Lesson Moderator for our UK based Physics teaching team!




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking Live Lesson Moderators to support our teachers during livestreamed lessons. Moderators play a key role in ensuring lessons run smoothly by responding to student questions, clarifying educational content, and managing general queries.

This is a fully remote role, ideal for current university students, those on a gap year, or recent graduates who enjoy working with students in real time and contributing to high-quality online education. We offer both full-time and part-time opportunities, with evening availability required to support live lessons.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Participating in the live chat of livestreamed Physics lessons to provide subject-related responses to student questions as well as adherence to MyEdSpace codes of conduct.

 - Providing real-time support to teachers to help lessons run smoothly.

 - Assisting students with basic technical or access issues during live sessions.

 - Min number of sessions a week: 3 x 1 hr sessions




WHO WE'RE SEEKING

 - Grade A*/A at A-Level in Physics.

 - Strong subject knowledge and the ability to communicate clearly and concisely.

 - Excellent written communication skills, particularly in fast-paced, live environments.

 - Calm, responsible, and confident in enforcing behavioural guidelines when required.

 - Comfortable multitasking while maintaining attention to detail.

 - Available to work 3–5 days per week, with flexibility to cover evening lessons.



✨ Our values

PUT INTEGRITY FIRST Honesty matters. Tell the truth, and be straight-up. Be transparent and do the right thing. This builds respect and reliability for our students, families and team members.

KNOW YOUR COORDINATES Understand where you are and where you want to get to. Know your strengths and acknowledge your gaps. Think from first principles. Question things, and never pretend to know what you don't.

RAISE THE BAR Own it and get it done. Do better. Iterate quickly and seek feedback. Deliver real value. Be excellent and lift others up. Share learnings and help your teammates improve.

LOVE TO BE WRONG Push boundaries. Be outside your comfort zone. Naturally you'll get some things wrong and that's okay. Embrace feedback. Learn from it and get closer to excellence.

WIN TOGETHER To achieve our mission, we have to work together. We all have a role to play, so help each other get there. We’re stronger as a team so inspire, support and respect each other.



🙋 Why you’ll love working here

We’re a team on a mission to transform education for the better. Joining MES means you’ll be part of something ambitious, fast-moving, and full of purpose. Here’s what you can expect:

 - The chance to make a real impact: your work directly shapes the future of education.

 - A fast-paced and high-growth environment where ideas move quickly and careers accelerate.

 - A collaborative, supportive culture: we’re head quartered in the UK, but we’re a global team with colleagues in 15+ countries, bringing a rich mix of perspectives and energy.

We're hiring our first Student Content Creator for our US team!

If you're a college student or on a gap year, holding an A grade in Calculus I, II, or III, and achieving a 4 or 5 on the AP Calculus AB or BC Exam, this opportunity is for you.




ABOUT THE ROLE

 - Open to current college students and individuals on a gap year.

 - Key responsibilities involve crafting course materials such as homework questions, transforming lesson slides into workbooks, and organizing video solutions.

 - This position offers full remote work with flexible scheduling.





RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Transforming lessons into workbooks for student use.

 - Generating homework questions to complement educators' teachings.

 - Archiving teachers' video solutions post-filming.

 - Updating the online platform for easy student access before lessons.

 - Supporting the moderation of live chat during livestream lessons.





WHO WE'RE SEEKING

 - High school achiever with a 4 or 5 on the AP Calculus AB or BC exam.

 - College/university achiever with "A" grades in Calculus I, II, or III.

 - Capable of collaborative work with educators and adjusting to curriculum requirements.

 - Strong organizational and communication abilities.

 - Dedicated, adaptable, and enthusiastic about educational content development.





KEY DETAILS

 - Title: Student Content Creator

 - Reporting to: Senior Math Teacher

 - Location: Remote

 - Start date: ASAP

 - Duration: Ongoing

 - Pay: $13.50/hour

We’re a team on a mission to transform education for the better. Joining MES means you’ll be part of something ambitious, fast-moving, and full of purpose. Here’s what you can expect:

source_jobs.title: Senior Math Teacher
source_jobs.description:
MyEdSpace is an education technology platform that connects the best teachers on the planet with the students who need them the most - wherever they are, whatever their means.

Backed by $15m in Series A funding from some of the leading investors in the space, we're ready to scale - and we want you to help us build and scale MyEdSpace as part of a collaborative and ambitious team.

We're on a mission to make a world-class education accessible to all.

We believe that every child deserves access to quality education, regardless of geography or socioeconomic background, to achieve their dreams and build a brighter future.

If you’re motivated by solving meaningful problems and creating real impact through your work on a societal level and make a real impact on the world - we’d love to hear from you!

Key details

 - Title: Senior Math Teacher

 - Reporting to: CEO

 - Location: Remote

 - Start date: July 2026

 - Duration: Ongoing

 - Pay: Up to $120,000 pa plus performance bonus




💼 THE ROLE:

Our UK business has been active since 2022, and is growing fast. We're building on this traction with a full-scale launch in the USA in 2026. We're already live, with Math courses running each week, but this is just the start.

We're now looking for more world-class teachers to join the team and expand our offering as we scale across the country. This isn’t a typical teaching role, and our platform will give you the ability to reach literally millions of students each week. If you love teaching and want to make a real difference, this is the role for you.




⚙️ WHAT YOU’LL DO

As a Senior Math Teacher at MyEdSpace, you will:

 - Deliver high-quality math lessons via live, interactive online teaching.

 - Design and develop a full curriculum from scratch, ensuring it meets rigorous academic standards, including AP, SAT, ACT and standard learning curricula up to high school level.

 - Create engaging educational content for YouTube, TikTok, and Instagram to support student learning.

 - Refine teaching methods based on student feedback and performance data.

 - Represent MyEdSpace in the U.S. and globally, growing a dedicated student following through social media and outreach.

 - Collaborate with our Marketing Team on innovative campaigns to expand MyEdSpace’s reach.
   
   


🙋 WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR

You’re a match for this role if you:

 - Hold a bachelor’s degree or higher in Mathematics or a closely related field, achieving a 3.5 GPA (or equivalent) or higher

 - Achieved top grades in your subject at college and high school

 - Are a dedicated and hardworking individual

 - Possess a valid U.S. teaching certification in secondary math education (state-specific certifications accepted).

 - Have at least 5 years of teaching experience, including AP-level instruction.

 - Preferably have experience leading a math department or overseeing curriculum for middle or high school students.

 - Have experience as an AP, IB, or standardized test scorer (preferred but not required).

 - Are authorized to work in the United States.

 - Are comfortable developing your personal brand on social media—we will support and provide the tools needed to succeed, but you must be open to the idea of becoming an influencer.

 - Possess excellent communication and interpersonal skills.

 - Are driven to develop personally, contributing to a transformative educational venture.




🔩 DETAILS:

 - Location: Remote, for now. We expect to open a US office in 2026 but for now our team in the US is remote.

 - Reporting to: Co-Founder / CEO

 - Compensation: $120k base salary with additional performance bonus (up to $25k pa) plus meaningful equity.



✨ Our values

PUT INTEGRITY FIRST Honesty matters. Tell the truth, and be straight-up. Be transparent and do the right thing. This builds respect and reliability for our students, families and team members.

KNOW YOUR COORDINATES Understand where you are and where you want to get to. Know your strengths and acknow

source_jobs.title: Lead Practitioner of Economics
source_jobs.description:
MyEdSpace is an education technology platform that connects the best teachers on the planet with the students who need them the most - wherever they are, whatever their means.

Backed by $15m in Series A funding from some of the leading investors in the space, we're ready to scale - and we want you to help us build and scale MyEdSpace as part of a collaborative and ambitious team.

We're on a mission to make a world-class education accessible to all.

We believe that every child deserves access to quality education, regardless of geography or socioeconomic background, to achieve their dreams and build a brighter future.

If you’re motivated by solving meaningful problems and creating real impact through your work on a societal level and make a real impact on the world - we’d love to hear from you!

Step into the future of education as MyEdSpace's dedicated Economics Teacher for Key Stages 3, 4 and 5. This position is a full-time role with ~6-12 lessons delivered per week.



Differences vs. A Traditional Teaching Role?

Like a normal teaching role, you will be responsible for designing and delivering your subject’s curriculum via interactive lessons, multiple times per week. We just do this via online live streams instead of in person.

Here’s the twist… We turn you into an educational influencer on social media. For context, our Maths teacher currently has over 2.4 million social media followers. As such, you will be a leading face of the company and need to be comfortable building this online presence. You will partake in marketing efforts, including building social media content and being featured in adverts to promote MyEdSpace and your course. You will report directly to our Founding Team.

If you’re excited to change from a traditional school environment to a startup, where you can use technology to make a larger-scale impact, we want to hear from you!

Your Responsibilities:

 - Plan and deliver engaging online lessons via live streams - interact with your class and answer questions as they arise

 - Prepare all course materials including full curriculum plans, lesson plans, presentations and homeworks etc

 - Create engaging educational content for social media (we will help at every step of the way, don’t worry)

 - Collaborate with our Marketing Team on innovative ad campaigns

 - Communicate with students and parents to refine our services

 - Support our Co-Founders in various other company initiatives



Ideally, You:

 - Hold a degree (or higher) in Economics or a related field, achieving a 2:1 or above

 - Achieved top grades at A-level, achieving A/A* in your subject at A-level

 - Are willing to work in London (we are willing to consider a hybrid working model for outstanding candidates)

 - Are a dedicated and hardworking individual

 - Have a minimum of 5 years teaching experience and QTS.

 - Have experience teaching OCR/ AQA Economics at A-level

 - Authorised to work in the United Kingdom

 - Are willing to develop your personal brand on social media - we will help and give you all the tools needed to succeed but you have to be comfortable with the idea of becoming an influencer

 - Possess excellent communication and interpersonal skills

 - Is driven to develop personally, contributing to a transformative educational venture



✨ Our values

PUT INTEGRITY FIRST Honesty matters. Tell the truth, and be straight-up. Be transparent and do the right thing. This builds respect and reliability for our students, families and team members.

source_jobs.title: Sales Exec (Inbound B2C)
source_jobs.description:
MyEdSpace is an education technology platform that connects the best teachers on the planet with the students who need them the most - wherever they are, whatever their means.

Backed by $15m in Series A funding from some of the leading investors in the space, we're ready to scale - and we want you to help us build and scale MyEdSpace as part of a collaborative and ambitious team.

We're on a mission to make a world-class education accessible to all.

We believe that every child deserves access to quality education, regardless of geography or socioeconomic background, to achieve their dreams and build a brighter future.

If you’re motivated by solving meaningful problems and creating real impact through your work on a societal level and make a real impact on the world - we’d love to hear from you!




💼 THE ROLE:

You’ll be calling warm leads (parents who have already engaged with our ads) and closing sales for educational courses priced between £80 and £1500. If you’re eager to learn, aligned with our mission to transform education, and motivated by helping students succeed, this is the perfect role for you.

You’ll report directly to one of our Team Leaders, offering a strong opportunity for professional growth and development. Working hours are flexible, with the opportunity to work weekdays or weekends, daytime or evenings depending on your preference.




⚙️ WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Close warm leads - Convert customers who've clicked our ads into paying customers through consultative phone sales conversations. No cold calling here!

 - Pitch tailored packages - Present our educational courses by understanding each family's needs and matching them with the right solution

 - Own your sales pipeline - Manage follow-ups, overcome objections, and drive deals to completion while maintaining accurate CRM records

 - Hit ambitious monthly targets - Consistently meet and exceed lead conversion goals to maximise your (uncapped) commission earnings

 - Represent our mission - Act as the voice of MyEdSpace, building trust with families and demonstrating how we can transform their children's educational outcomes




🙋 WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Experienced selling to the UK market

 - Preferably 1+ years of outbound telemarketing experience.

 - Excellent communication (both verbal and written) and negotiation skills

 - Ability to work to set targets and exceed them

 - Great with customers, comfortable engaging and building rapport

 - A team player with good time management skills and the ability to multi-task

 - The ability to also work independently and manage a large workload

 - Committed, flexible and willing to learn



Location: Chancery Lane, London, UK

Contract Type: Fixed Term Contract for 3 months

Compensation: £26.5k (pro rata) plus uncapped commission



✨ Our values

PUT INTEGRITY FIRST Honesty matters. Tell the truth, and be straight-up. Be transparent and do the right thing. This builds respect and reliability for our students, families and team members.

KNOW YOUR COORDINATES Understand where you are and where you want to get to. Know your strengths and acknowledge your gaps. Think from first principles. Question things, and never pretend to know what you don't.

RAISE THE BAR Own it and get it done. Do better. Iterate quickly and seek feedback. Deliver real value. Be excellent and lift others up. Share learnings and help your teammates improve.

LOVE TO BE WRONG Push boundaries. Be outside your comfort zone. Naturally you'll get some things wrong and that's okay. Embrace feedback. Learn from it and get closer to excellence.

WIN TOGETHER To achieve our mission, we have to work together. We all have a role to play, so help each other get there. We’re stronger as a team so inspire, support and respect each other.



🙋 Why you’ll love working here

We’re a team on a mission to transform education for the better. Joining MES means you’ll be part of something ambitious, fast-moving, and full of purpose. Here’s what you ca

source_jobs.title: Live Lesson Moderator - English
source_jobs.description:
MyEdSpace is an education technology platform that connects the best teachers on the planet with the students who need them the most - wherever they are, whatever their means.

Backed by $15m in Series A funding from some of the leading investors in the space, we're ready to scale - and we want you to help us build and scale MyEdSpace as part of a collaborative and ambitious team.

We're on a mission to make a world-class education accessible to all.

We believe that every child deserves access to quality education, regardless of geography or socioeconomic background, to achieve their dreams and build a brighter future.

If you’re motivated by solving meaningful problems and creating real impact through your work on a societal level and make a real impact on the world - we’d love to hear from you!

We're hiring a Live Lesson Moderator for our UK based English teaching team!




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking Live Lesson Moderators to support our teachers during livestreamed lessons. Moderators play a key role in ensuring lessons run smoothly by responding to student questions, clarifying educational content, and managing general queries.

This is a fully remote role, ideal for current university students, those on a gap year, or recent graduates who enjoy working with students in real time and contributing to high-quality online education. We offer both full-time and part-time opportunities, with evening availability required to support live lessons.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Participating in the live chat of livestreamed English lessons to provide subject-related responses to student questions as well as adherence to MyEdSpace codes of conduct.

 - Providing real-time support to teachers to help lessons run smoothly.

 - Assisting students with basic technical or access issues during live sessions.

 - Min number of sessions a week: 3 x 1 hr sessions




WHO WE'RE SEEKING

 - Grade A*/A at A-Level in English.

 - Strong subject knowledge and the ability to communicate clearly and concisely.

 - Excellent written communication skills, particularly in fast-paced, live environments.

 - Calm, responsible, and confident in enforcing behavioural guidelines when required.

 - Comfortable multitasking while maintaining attention to detail.

 - Available to work 3–5 days per week, with flexibility to cover evening lessons.



✨ Our values

PUT INTEGRITY FIRST Honesty matters. Tell the truth, and be straight-up. Be transparent and do the right thing. This builds respect and reliability for our students, families and team members.

KNOW YOUR COORDINATES Understand where you are and where you want to get to. Know your strengths and acknowledge your gaps. Think from first principles. Question things, and never pretend to know what you don't.

RAISE THE BAR Own it and get it done. Do better. Iterate quickly and seek feedback. Deliver real value. Be excellent and lift others up. Share learnings and help your teammates improve.

LOVE TO BE WRONG Push boundaries. Be outside your comfort zone. Naturally you'll get some things wrong and that's okay. Embrace feedback. Learn from it and get closer to excellence.

WIN TOGETHER To achieve our mission, we have to work together. We all have a role to play, so help each other get there. We’re stronger as a team so inspire, support and respect each other.



🙋 Why you’ll love working here

We’re a team on a mission to transform education for the better. Joining MES means you’ll be part of something ambitious, fast-moving, and full of purpose. Here’s what you can expect:

 - The chance to make a real impact: your work directly shapes the future of education.

 - A fast-paced and high-growth environment where ideas move quickly and careers accelerate.

 - A collaborative, supportive culture: we’re head quartered in the UK, but we’re a global team with colleagues in 15+ countries, bringing a rich mix of perspectives and energy.



We are committed to building a diverse team and strongly encourage applicat

Job Title: Inbound Sales Rep (B2C)

Manager: Sales Team Lead

Location: Remote

Contract Type: Contractor

Fixed Rate: $1,500 base plus uncapped commission

The role

We're looking for hungry, commission-driven sales professionals to help families transform their children's education through our online tutoring programs. You’ll be calling warm leads (they’ve clicked our ads) and closing sales for educational packages ranging from $99-$1,499.

This is not a customer service role - we need closers who love the thrill of winning deals and hitting targets.

 - Close warm leads - Convert parents who've clicked our ads into paying customers through consultative phone sales conversations

 - Pitch tailored tutoring packages - Present our educational programs ($99-$1,499) by understanding each family's needs and matching them with the right solution

 - Hit ambitious monthly targets - Consistently meet and exceed lead conversion goals to maximise your commission earnings

 - Represent our mission - Act as the voice of MyEdSpace, building trust with families and demonstrating how we can transform their children's educational outcomes.

 

Who we're looking for

 - 1+ years of sales experience, preferably in the UK or US market

 - Strong phone-based sales skills

 - C1/C2 English proficiency

 - A proven track record of closing deals, not just handling inbound enquiries

 - Reliable home office setup - computer (8GB+ RAM), headset with mic, stable wired internet

source_jobs.title: Recruitment Specialist
source_jobs.description:
MyEdSpace is an education technology platform that connects the best teachers on the planet with the students who need them the most - wherever they are, whatever their means.

Backed by $15m in Series A funding from some of the leading investors in the space, we're ready to scale - and we want you to help us build and scale MyEdSpace as part of a collaborative and ambitious team.

We're on a mission to make a world-class education accessible to all.

We believe that every child deserves access to quality education, regardless of geography or socioeconomic background, to achieve their dreams and build a brighter future.

If you’re motivated by solving meaningful problems and creating real impact through your work on a societal level and make a real impact on the world - we’d love to hear from you!


💼 THE ROLE:

With some incredibly exciting plans in store for 2026, we’re now looking for someone to join our growing People team and support with Talent Acquisition. We need someone who loves hiring, loves speaking with people and is exceptionally organised.

You’ll work on a wide range of roles across Technology (Engineering, Product, Data), Commercial (Sales, Customer Support, Founders Team) and Education (Teachers, Teaching Assistants) across the world (our team is spread across 20 countries and counting...) and partner closely with hiring managers to ensure we’re running a best-in-class recruitment operation.

This role is ideal for someone ready to play a huge part within a growing Talent Acquisition function. You’ll have the opportunity to play a huge role in the success of our business, starting with 100+ roles in 2026.




⚙️ WHAT YOU’LL DO

You will be expected to:

 - You’ll coordinate day to day hiring efforts; working with our Hiring Managers across each function:
   
   - Manage job postings, applicant tracking, scheduling, and candidate communication.
   
   - Support CV screening and run initial phone screens where appropriate.
   
   - Coordinate interviews and ensure a smooth candidate experience.
   
   - Lead on sourcing and outreach on key hires where required.

 - You’ll help build processes, leveraging our ATS (Ashby), automation and AI, to ensure we’re hiring the best talent, globally.

 - Handle routine admin such as offer paperwork, compliance checks, and reference coordination.

 - Feed back to management team on market trends, influencing people strategy where possible.

 - Support broader People Ops tasks during quieter recruiting periods.




🙋 WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR

This role is ideal for someone who has experience hiring a variety of different roles within a high-growth, global, technology business. You will be:

 - Exceptionally organised: With a very high attention to detail. You understand that the little things can make a huge difference.

 - A people person: You’re a clear, confident communicator and are able to build relationships quickly with candidates and team members alike.

 - Extremely tech-literate: You’ve got your finger on the pulse when it comes to best-in-class tooling, process and automation.

 - Data driven: You're comfortable working with data, understanding where your bottlenecks are and where you need to change things up.

 - A passionate recruiter: You’ll have a track record of delivery across multiple functions - ideally across technical and commercial.




🔩 DETAILS:

 - Location: Remote

 - Reporting to: Chief of Staff

 - Start Date: ASAP
